festivals.name: Timkat
festivals.description: Ethiopian Orthodox Epiphany is celebrated with colorful processions of the Ark of the Covenant replicas to water.

festivals.name: Meskel
festivals.description: The Finding of the True Cross festival draws tens of thousands to Meskel Square for a massive bonfire ceremony.

destinations.name: Addis Ababa
destinations.description: Africa's diplomatic capital has the National Museum housing Lucy's skeleton and Ethiopia's finest coffee ceremony culture.

destinations.name: Lalibela
destinations.description: Eleven medieval rock-hewn churches carved from living rock in the 12th century, still used for worship today.

destinations.name: Gondar
destinations.description: The royal city of castles where 17th-century emperors built a remarkable complex of fortified palaces.

destinations.name: Simien Mountains
destinations.description: Dramatic escarpments and plateaus at over 4,500m, home to gelada baboons found nowhere else on earth.

destinations.name: Danakil Depression
destinations.description: One of the hottest and lowest places on earth, with neon-yellow sulfur springs, lava lakes, and vast salt flats.

destinations.name: Omo Valley
destinations.description: Homeland of diverse tribal nations including the Hamar, Mursi, and Karo who maintain ancient traditions.

destinations.name: Harar
destinations.description: A UNESCO walled city of 82 mosques where the nightly hyena feeding is one of Africa's strangest spectacles.

destinations.name: Bale Mountains
destinations.description: Afroalpine plateau sheltering the Ethiopian wolf and mountain nyala in spectacular highland scenery.
